title: Eggplant Parmesan Flatbread
subtitle: with Shaved Zucchini and Arugula Salad
description: Mozzarella and sun dried tomatoes make this dish taste like an authentic eggplant parmesan. For slicing vegetables, a mandoline may be the most useful tool in your kitchen so if you have one, use it here for the eggplant and zucchini. If you like to grill, this is a good recipe to fire it up for. The eggplant can be grilled and after the flatbreads are assembled, simply leave them on the grill for a few moments with the lid closed to melt the cheese.
ingredients: ½ cup Follow Your Heart vegan mozzarella

steps.title: COOK THE EGGPLANT
steps.text: Trim and thinly slice the eggplant lengthwise. Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat and add 2 tbsp olive oil. Once hot, add the eggplant in one layer and cook until softened and browned, about 2 minutes each side. Once cooked, season with salt and pepper.

steps.title: BUILD THE FLATBREAD
steps.text: Set the oven to broil on low. Spread the roasted garlic on the naan with a spoon. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and then layer on the eggplant, overlapping slightly, until all of the slices are used.

steps.title: ADD THE TOPPINGS
steps.text: Pick the thyme leaves from the stems. Sprinkle the flatbreads with thyme leaves and sun dried tomatoes. Top with Follow Your Heart vegan mozzarella. Place the flatbreads onto a baking sheet. Broil until the vegan mozzarella is melted, about 4 to 6 minutes.

steps.title: MAKE THE SALAD
steps.text: Use a peeler or mandoline to make long ribbons of zucchini and add to large bowl. Roughly chop the hazelnuts. Add the chopped hazelnuts, arugula, white balsamic vinegar, and 1 tsp olive oil to the zucchini and season with salt and pepper. Toss salad well to combine.

steps.title: CUT THE FLATBREADS
steps.text: Once the flatbreads are finished, cut into quarters.

steps.title: BON APPETIT
steps.text: Serve the eggplant parmesan flatbreads on large plates alongside the shaved zucchini and arugula salad.

tips: Cook the eggplant in batches and add additional oil as needed.
Keep an eye on the flatbread while it's under the broiler.
